title: Ensure 'HTTPS Only' is set to 'On'

input_row.title: Ensure 'HTTPS Only' is set to 'On'
input_row.description: Azure App Service allows deployment slots to run under both HTTP and HTTPS by default. Deployment slots can be accessed by anyone using non-secure HTTP links by default. Non-secure HTTP requests can be restricted and all HTTP requests redirected to the secure HTTPS port. It is recommended to enforce HTTPS-only traffic.
input_row.rationale: Enabling HTTPS-only traffic will redirect all non-secure HTTP requests to HTTPS ports. HTTPS uses the TLS/SSL protocol to provide a secure connection which is both encrypted and authenticated. It is therefore important to support HTTPS for the security benefits.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings tab, under Platform settings, ensure that HTTPS Only is set to On. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each app and deployment slot. Audit from Azure CLI Run the following command to list apps: az webapp list For each app, run the following command to list deployment slots: az webapp deployment slot list --resource-group --name For each deployment slot, ensure that httpsOnly is set to true. Audit from PowerShell Run the following command to list apps: Get-AzWebApp Run the following command to list deployment slots for an app: Get-AzWebAppSlot -ResourceGroupName -Name Run the following command to get the deployment slot with a given name: $slot = Get-AzWebAppSlot -ResourceGroupName -Name -Slot Run the following command to get the HTTPS setting for the deployment slot: $slot.httpsOnly Ensure that the command returns True. Repeat for each app and deployment slot. Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: ae1b9a8c-dfce-4605-bd91-69213b4a26fc - Name: 'App Service app slots should only be accessible over HTTPS'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to App Services. 2. Click the name of an app. 3. Under Deployment, click Deployment slots. 4. Click the name of a deployment slot. 5. Under Settings, click Configuration. 6. In the General settings tab, under Platform settings, next to HTTPS Only, select the radio button next to On. 7. Click Save. 8. Click Continue. 9. Repeat steps 1-8 for each app and deployment slot requiring remediation. Remediate from Azure CLI For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to enable HTTPS Only: az resource update --resource-group --name /slots/ --resource-type "Microsoft.Web/sites" -- set properties.httpsOnly=true Remediate from PowerShell For each deployment slot requiring remediation, run the following command to enable HTTPS Only: Set-AzWebAppSlot -ResourceGroupName -Name - Slot -HttpsOnly $true
input_row.impact: When it is enabled, every incoming HTTP request is redirected to the HTTPS port. This means an extra level of security will be added to the HTTP requests made to the deployment slot.
input_row.default_value: HTTPS Only is disabled by default.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_app_service_deployment_slot_https_only_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **Azure API Calls / Azure SDK Methods:** 
  - Use the Azure SDK for Python's `WebSiteManagementClient` to interact with App Services and deployment slots.
  
- **Azure CLI Commands:**
  - To list all Azure web apps: `az webapp list`
  - To list deployment slots for a specific app: `az webapp deployment slot list --resource-group <resource-group> --name <app-name>`
  - To check the HTTPS-only setting for a deployment slot: `az webapp deployment slot show --resource-group <resource-group> --name <app-name> --slot <slot-name> --query httpsOnly`
  
- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:** 
  - All App Services under specified subscriptions/resource groups. 
  - All deployment slots for those App Services.

- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Verify that the `httpsOnly` property is set to `True` for each deployment slot.

- **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
  - For each deployment slot checked, a Pass is recorded if `httpsOnly` is `True`. A Fail is recorded if `httpsOnly` is `False`.

By following these guidelines, the audit approach ensures effective compliance checking of the "Ensure 'HTTPS Only' is set to 'On'" control in Azure App Services.
